body.claim: The two-point case holds in logarithmically averaged form: Σ_{n≤x} λ(n)λ(n+h)/n = o(log x) for every fixed h ≠ 0 — short of the unaveraged statement, and of k ≥ 3.
refs: T. Tao, The logarithmically averaged Chowla and Elliott conjectures for two-point correlations, Forum Math. Pi 4 (2016), arXiv:1509.05422

body.claim: All ODD-order correlations vanish in logarithmically averaged form; the logarithmically averaged even orders k ≥ 4, and every unaveraged order, remain open.
refs: T. Tao, J. Teräväinen, Odd order cases of the logarithmically averaged Chowla conjecture, J. Théor. Nombres Bordeaux 30(3) (2018)

body.claim: The best dimension-free bound is 2t − log*t (Bukh 2016), a lower-order improvement on the original 2t−1 — still linear in t, not the conjectured O(√t).
refs: J. Beck, T. Fiala, "Integer-making" theorems, Discrete Appl. Math. 3 (1981) — discrepancy ≤ 2t−1
B. Bukh, An improvement of the Beck–Fiala theorem, Combin. Probab. Comput. (2016), arXiv:1306.6081

body.claim: Allowing a dependence on the ground-set size n gives O(√(t log n)) (Banaszczyk 1998) — √t-shaped in t, but not the dimension-free bound the conjecture asks for.
refs: W. Banaszczyk, Balancing vectors and Gaussian measures of n-dimensional convex bodies, Random Structures Algorithms 12 (1998)
An Improved Bound for the Beck-Fiala Conjecture, arXiv:2508.01937 (2025) — O(√(t log log n)) once t ≥ log⁵n

body.claim: Given a THIRD ℚ-independent y₃, the conclusion is a theorem: one of the six numbers e^{xᵢyⱼ} is transcendental (six exponentials theorem). The 2×2 case is exactly where that proof fails.
refs: S. Lang, Introduction to Transcendental Numbers (1966)
K. Ramachandra, Contributions to the theory of transcendental numbers (1968)

body.claim: With a third prime the answer is known unconditionally: if 2^x, 3^x and 5^x are all integers then x is an integer — the six exponentials theorem applied at (y₁,y₂,y₃) = (log 2, log 3, log 5).
refs: M. Waldschmidt, Diophantine Approximation on Linear Algebraic Groups (2000)
